rationale.R1.2: "Solid" implicates checking whether conclusions follow from premises — notably the claim that integrated coupling uniquely satisfies both commitment and arbitrage, and the gradient-dominance argument dismissing hyperparameter tuning as a remedy for soft-penalty approaches.
rationale.R1.3: Multiple strong empirical claims are asserted ("perfect consistency," no other strategy achieves both properties) without inline evidence; verifying these are grounded in reported results is central to solidity.
rationale.R1.6: "Solid" plausibly includes prose quality; the passage contains run-on comma chains and dense, clause-heavy sentences that may obscure the argument and weaken reader confidence.

parsed.clarification_question: Is your primary concern verifying that the logical arguments and empirical claims are sound and adequately qualified (substantive technical review), or improving the clarity and readability of the prose (writing polish)?
